conditions.notes: Used to determine the size of the polybutadiene blocks. PS blocks are chromatographically invisible.
conditions.evidence_text: The critical point of PS is obtained at an eluent composition of THF/n-hexane 43.4% : 56.6% by volume, all PS samples being eluted in one peak. At this eluent composition, PB is eluted in the SEC mode.
conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical point of adsorption of polystyrene
conditions.detector: Differential refractometer, UV detector, FTIR (LC Transform)

conditions.notes: Used to determine the size of the polystyrene blocks. PB blocks are chromatographically invisible. Solubility limit for PB homopolymer is ~30,000 g/mol, but block copolymers are soluble.
conditions.evidence_text: The critical point of adsorption on a RP-18 stationary phase corresponds to a mobile phase composition of MEK/cyclohexane 98% :2% by volume... At the critical point of adsorption for PB, PS would elute in the SEC mode.
conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical point of adsorption of polybutadiene
conditions.detector: Differential refractometer, UV detector, FTIR (LC Transform)
